label: Mixture-of-experts models

node.label: Mixture-of-experts models
node.definition: A mixture-of-experts model routes each input or token through a selected subset of specialized subnetworks, expanding parameter capacity at controlled compute cost.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Mixture-of-experts models is to connect Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts, Affine transforms & dense layers to downstream capabilities including Distributed, mixed-precision & memory-efficient training, Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference.
examples: substrate for Distributed, mixed-precision & memory-efficient training
substrate for Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts, Affine transforms & dense layers.

dependencies.rationale: Mixture-of-experts models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts.
Mixture-of-experts models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
Mixture-of-experts models rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Distributed systems & networking.
